= Contents =
    - [[#configuration|configuration]]
    - [[#usage|usage]]
        - [[#usage#generate|generate]]
        - [[#usage#list|list]]
        - [[#usage#edit|edit]]
        - [[#usage#private keys|private keys]]
        - [[#usage#public keys|public keys]]
        - [[#usage#encrypt/decrypt|encrypt/decrypt]]

    GnuPG allows you to encrypt and sign your data and communications; it features a versatile key management system, along with access modules for all kinds of public key directories.
    GnuPG, also known as GPG, is a command line tool with features for easy integration with other applications.
    A wealth of frontend applications and libraries are available. GnuPG also provides support for S/MIME and Secure Shell (ssh).

= configuration =
default storage is `~/.gnupg` and can be modified with `$GNUPGHOME` env variable
default config files are `~/.gnupg/gpg.conf` and `~/.gnupg/dirmngr.conf`
= usage =
== generate ==
generate key `gpg --full-gen-key`
generate revocation certificate `gpg --gen-revoke --armor --output=revocation_certificate.asc user-id`
== list ==
`gpg --list-keys`
== edit ==
`gpg --edit-key <user-id>`
== private keys ==
export `gpg --export-secret-keys --armor <user-id> > privkey.asc`
== public keys ==
export `gpg --output public.key --armor --export user-id`
import `gpg --import public.key`
== encrypt/decrypt ==
encrypt `gpg --recipient user-id --encrypt doc`
decrypt `gpg --output doc --decrypt doc.gpg`
